reasoning: I'm voting on the same principle I voted Congress in 2019—it's the only party on this ballot making a constitutional argument against the BJP's project, even though I know the machinery won't change. Shantanu Thakur is the 'CAA-promise candidate,' and I cannot vote for a party that has spent five years reshaping the republic's character to exclude citizens by religion, no matter how much it performs delivery to the Matua community. TMC is morally hollowed and complicit, but they're not ideologically remaking the constitutional order. The left is spent, yes—but INC, however weak, is at least contesting the framework itself rather than competing within it. My vote won't matter structurally; the machinery will work the same way regardless. But I'd rather cast it against the thing that frightens me most than surrender to cynicism.
primary_drivers: Constitutional alarm: CAA as a framework that makes religion a criterion for citizenship
Secular-left principle: the machinery is broken, but BJP's ideological project is categorically worse
Consistency with 2019: INC as the least-compromised available option, even knowing it won't change structural outcomes
